projects.title: Spongebot
projects.desc:
Spongebot

A SpongeBob SquarePants-themed Arduino robot built as a personal gift, controlled wirelessly via a custom Android app over Bluetooth. A passion project that combines embedded hardware, custom industrial design, and mobile app development into one character-accurate, fully functional robot.

Overview

Spongebot started as a personal project — a one-of-a-kind SpongeBob robot built and gifted to someone special. Rather than using an off-the-shelf controller, a dedicated Android app was designed and built specifically for it, giving the robot a polished user experience that matched its personality. The result is a fully self-contained system: power it on, open the app, and it connects automatically.

Key Features

📱 Dedicated Android App     A custom-built Android application (.apk) designed exclusively for Spongebot. The app auto-connects to the robot via Bluetooth on launch — no manual pairing steps needed after the first setup.

🔵 Bluetooth Wireless Control     Communicates over Bluetooth Classic using an HC-05 module. A built-in LED indicator on the robot provides clear connection feedback — blinking red means not connected, steady red means connected and ready.

🎨 Custom Physical Design     The robot body was designed and built to resemble SpongeBob, with a custom layout crafted to match the character's proportions and look. Design files are included in the repository under the sponge layout folder.

⚡ Arduino-Powered Firmware     The robot's onboard logic runs on Arduino firmware written in C/C++, handling Bluetooth command parsing and motor/actuator control. Circuit schematics and Fritzing diagrams are included for full reproducibility.

Tech Stack

CategoryDetails
MicrocontrollerArduino
FirmwareC / C++ (Arduino IDE)
WirelessBluetooth Classic (HC-05)
Mobile AppAndroid (Custom APK)
Circuit DesignFritzing
Physical DesignCustom layout / enclosure

projects.title: Aero — U-Robot Link Universal Robot Controller
projects.desc:
Aero — U-Robot Link

A universal Android robot controller app published on the Google Play Store, designed to eliminate the need to build a custom remote-control app for every new robotics project. One app. Every platform.

Overview

Aero was built to solve a recurring problem in the maker and robotics community — every new project meant reinventing the same controller UI from scratch. Aero replaces that by providing a single, polished Android app that speaks a simple serial protocol over Bluetooth, BLE, or WiFi, and automatically translates it into the correct binary protocol when connecting to LEGO MINDSTORMS hardware.

Whether you're driving an Arduino car, monitoring IoT sensors, or controlling a LEGO EV3 robot — Aero handles it all without writing a single line of controller code.

Key Features

📡 Multi-Protocol Connectivity     Supports Bluetooth Classic (HC-05/HC-06), BLE (HM-10, ESP32), and WiFi TCP (ESP8266, ESP32) — all from the same device connection screen. Includes auto-reconnect on startup for frequently used devices.

🤖 LEGO MINDSTORMS Support (NXT & EV3)     Connects natively to LEGO NXT and EV3 bricks using the official LEGO Direct Commands protocol over Bluetooth — no custom firmware, no ev3dev, no leJOS required. Motor port mapping, directional inversion, brake-on-stop, deadband, and max power are all configurable from within the app.

🎮 10 Control Modes     Includes a fully customizable button grid, dual joystick with differential drive math, tilt control via accelerometer, gamepad support for PS4/PS5/Xbox controllers, RGB color picker, serial terminal, countdown timer with auto-send, voice control via Google Speech Recognition, a live IP camera viewer (MJPEG), and a full IoT sensor dashboard.

📊 IoT Sensor Dashboard     Parses SENSOR:key:value messages from any connected device and auto-generates live widgets — gauge, chart, bar, LED indicator, compass, and more — with color-coding, threshold alerts, drag-to-reposition layout, and profile saving. Supports up to 500 readings per sensor in memory.

🌐 Built-in Web Dashboard     Runs a local WebSocket server on port 8080 that lets any browser on the same WiFi view live sensor data, sparkline charts, history tables, and CSV exports — no cloud, no subscription, fully offline.

📱 Live Camera Stream     Streams MJPEG video from ESP32-CAM or any compatible IP camera directly in the app, operating independently from the robot control connection.

🧩 Saved Profiles & Dark Mode     Multiple named profiles per device for switching between control layouts (e.g. "Drive Mode", "Arm Mode", "IoT Mode"). Full light/dark theme support for outdoor and low-light use.

Tech Stack

CategoryDetails
PlatformAndroid (Java / Android SDK, API 24+)
ConnectivityBluetooth Classic · BLE · WiFi TCP
LEGO ProtocolLEGO Direct Commands (NXT & EV3 binary spec)
CameraMJPEG stream rendering
VoiceGoogle Speech Recognition API
Web ServerLocal WebSocket + HTTP server
DistributionGoogle Play Store

projects.title: QR Attendance System
projects.desc:
QR-SMS Attendance System

A desktop attendance management application built with C# and Windows Forms, designed to streamline student check-in/check-out using QR code scanning and automated SMS notifications to guardians.

Overview

This project addresses a common pain point in school administration — manual attendance tracking is slow, error-prone, and leaves parents out of the loop. The QR-SMS Attendance System replaces that process with a real-time, camera-based solution that logs attendance and immediately notifies a student's guardian via SMS the moment they arrive or are dismissed.

Key Features

📷 QR Code Scanning     Uses the device's webcam to continuously scan for student QR codes in real time, with a live camera feed, bounding-box overlay on successful scans, and intelligent decode throttling to keep CPU usage low.

📋 Student Database Integration     Loads a student roster directly from an Excel spreadsheet (.xlsx), automatically mapping columns for name, LRN, phone number, address, year level, section, track/strand, guardian, and more. No manual re-entry required.

📲 Automated SMS via Webhook     On a successful scan, the app builds a personalized SMS message and dispatches it to a connected Android device via a webhook endpoint. Messages are fully customizable with template placeholders and include a 5-second cooldown per student to prevent duplicate sends.

⏱ On-Time / Late Detection     Compares the scan timestamp against a configurable scheduled time and automatically stamps each log entry as On Time, Late, or Dismissed for OUT scans.

📁 CSV Attendance Logging     Every scan is written to a persistent attendance.csv file with full student details, timestamp, and IN/OUT status — ready for reporting or further processing.

🔲 QR Code Generator     Built-in tool to generate and save QR codes from any text input, making it easy to produce student ID QR codes directly within the same application.

Tech Stack

CategoryTools Used
Language / FrameworkC# · .NET · Windows Forms
Computer VisionOpenCvSharp
QR Decoding / EncodingZXing.Net
Excel I/OClosedXML
MessagingHTTP Webhook (JSON → Android SMS Bridge)
Data StorageCSV · Plain Text
